secrets.text: I still replay one argument from 2018 and wish I had apologized sooner.
secrets.author: Anonymous

secrets.text: I laugh at work all day and then sit in my car for ten extra minutes before driving home because I don't want to be alone yet.
secrets.author: Anonymous

secrets.text: I paid off my mom's utility bill without telling her and she called it a miracle.
secrets.author: quiethelper

secrets.text: I keep a note in my phone of things I never say out loud.
secrets.author: Anonymous

secrets.text: I told my brother I'd visit more often, but I keep postponing because I don't know how to talk to him after everything.
secrets.author: Anonymous

secrets.text: I moved to a city full of people and somehow feel lonelier than I did in my hometown.
secrets.author: N

secrets.text: I started leaving handwritten encouragement notes in library books and found one of mine posted online by a stranger who needed it.
secrets.author: papertrail

secrets.text: I keep wondering if everyone else is also pretending to know what they're doing.
secrets.author: Anonymous

secrets.text: I avoided a friend for months because I was ashamed to admit I needed help.
secrets.author: A.

secrets.text: I smile in every group photo lately, but I've been struggling to sleep most nights.
secrets.author: Anonymous

secrets.text: I paid for the coffee of the person behind me, and now I do it every Friday because it reminds me the world can still be kind.
secrets.author: Anonymous

secrets.text: I wrote down my five-year plan and realized I was chasing someone else's definition of success.
secrets.author: Anonymous
